title: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Koyfin Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Koyfin (4.3★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Koyfin — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Koyfin?

Koyfin is a financial data platform designed to democratize access to institutional-grade market research and analysis tools. Founded in 2016, it offers Bloomberg-quality data at roughly 1/50th of the cost, making it accessible to retail investors, fundamental analysts, and portfolio managers who need serious analytical firepower without the enterprise price tag. With a 4.3/5 rating and pricing starting at free, Koyfin covers 50+ global exchanges with real-time charting, custom screening tools, and a comprehensive news feed. Whether you're building custom dashboards, tracking fundamental metrics, or monitoring alerts across thousands of stocks and options, Koyfin serves traders who want data depth without compromise.

How to Create Your Koyfin Account

Getting started with Koyfin takes about 10 minutes and requires minimal friction. Follow these steps:

  • Visit koyfin.com and click "Sign Up" — You can register using your email address or connect via Google/Apple authentication for faster setup.
  • Enter your email and create a password — Koyfin doesn't require verification of trading experience or account size, so you can start immediately on the free tier.
  • Choose your subscription plan — Opt for Free initially to test the platform, or upgrade to Plus ($27.50/mo), Pro ($45/mo), or Enterprise ($59/mo) if you need premium features.
  • Verify your email — Check your inbox and confirm your email address. This typically takes seconds.
  • Complete your profile — Add your name and select your role (retail investor, analyst, fund manager, etc.). This helps Koyfin tailor your experience and is optional on the free tier.
  • Accept terms and log in — You're ready to access the platform immediately. No trading account linking is required since Koyfin is research-only.

Unlike Bloomberg or other professional platforms, Koyfin doesn't require proof of institutional affiliation or employment. The entire process is self-serve, making it ideal if you want to evaluate the platform before committing money.

Setting Up Koyfin for the First Time

Once you log in, you'll see the main dashboard — a blank canvas where you build your workspace. Unlike cluttered platforms, Koyfin starts minimal and grows with your needs.

Dashboard Customization: Koyfin's default view shows a blank workspace with a top navigation bar and left sidebar. Add widgets by clicking the "+" button: add watchlists, charts, screeners, or fundamental data cards. Drag widgets to resize and arrange them. Your layout saves automatically, so your setup persists across sessions.

Connect Your Data Feed: If you're on the Pro tier or above, you can integrate Excel exports directly into your spreadsheets. There's no direct broker connection like Robinhood or TD Ameritrade, since Koyfin is analysis-only — but you can pull data via API (paid tier) or manually export CSVs for your trading system.

Set Your Market Preferences: Go to Settings and define your default currency, timezone, and which exchanges you follow most (US stocks, international equities, options, etc.). Enable notifications for your most-watched tickers.

Create Your First Watchlist: In the left sidebar, click "Watchlists" and create a list for your investment thesis (e.g., "Semiconductor Deep Dives" or "Value Screens"). Add 10-20 tickers to start. You'll immediately see fundamental metrics, price performance, and news for each holding.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Advanced Screening Tool

Koyfin's screener lets you filter across 500+ fundamental and technical metrics simultaneously. Unlike basic screeners on Yahoo Finance, you can combine P/E ratios, revenue growth, insider transactions, short interest, and custom indicators in one query. For example: "Find companies with P/E under 15, revenue growth over 10%, and insider buying in the last month." Run the screen and get actionable results instantly. Free tier screens are limited to 20 filters; Pro adds unlimited filters and saved screen history.

2. Custom Indicators and Charting

Build your own technical indicators using Koyfin's formula builder, or use 100+ pre-built indicators (moving averages, Bollinger Bands, RSI, MACD, etc.). Draw directly on charts, combine multiple timeframes, and overlay fundamental data (earnings, dividends) on price action. The Pro tier unlocks unlimited custom indicators; the Free tier is limited to standard indicators only.

3. News Feed and Alerts

Koyfin aggregates news from 1,000+ sources globally and allows you to filter by ticker, sentiment, and source credibility. Set custom alerts for price movements, earnings announcements, analyst downgrades, insider trades, or news keywords. Get notifications via email or in-app. This is particularly powerful combined with your watchlists — monitor multiple positions without checking multiple websites.

4. Fundamental Analysis Dashboard

View company financials in interactive dashboards: income statements, balance sheets, cash flow, and custom metrics. Compare multiple companies side-by-side. Koyfin shows historical trends (5-10 years) and consensus estimates, allowing you to spot deteriorating margins or misses before they happen. This is available across all tiers but with higher historical depth on paid plans.

5. Performance Analytics

Track your portfolio performance against benchmarks (S&P 500, Russell 2000, Nasdaq, custom indices). View attribution analysis: which holdings drove returns, which lagged. On Pro and above, you can upload historical trades to calculate precise metrics like Sharpe ratio, maximum drawdown, and win rate. Free tier shows basic performance tracking only.

6. API Access (Enterprise Only)

The Enterprise tier ($59/mo) includes API access for programmatic data pulls. Connect Koyfin to your own systems, backtesting engines, or internal dashboards. You can build automated workflows: pull earnings data hourly, feed it to your model, trigger alerts. This is overkill for most retail traders but critical for anyone running systematic strategies.

7. Excel Integration (Pro and Above)

Export data tables directly into Excel with live links. Your spreadsheet updates when new data publishes. Perfect for building custom models or reporting dashboards without manual copy-paste.

Koyfin Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free ($0/mo)

The free tier is genuinely useful for casual research: basic screener (20 filters), pre-built technical indicators, news feed, and fundamental data with 2-year history. Delayed data (15-20 minute lag). Recommended for beginners learning fundamental analysis or confirming the platform fits your workflow before paying.

Plus ($27.50/mo)

Adds real-time data, unlimited screener filters, advanced charting with custom indicators, and email alerts. No API, no Excel integration. Best for intermediate traders who want real-time quotes and deeper filtering but don't need to feed data into other systems. At $330/year, this is the sweet spot for most individual investors.

Pro ($45/mo)

Includes everything in Plus, plus Excel integration, 10-year fundamental history, portfolio performance tracking, and advanced notifications. No API access. Recommended for portfolio managers, fundamental analysts, or traders building custom models in spreadsheets. The Excel integration alone saves 5+ hours per month if you're modeling companies.

Enterprise ($59/mo)

Full API access, white-label options, and dedicated support. Overkill for retail traders unless you're running an algorithmic strategy or internal research system. Best for small hedge funds or prop traders with custom infrastructure.

Bottom line: Start free, upgrade to Plus ($27.50) if you need real-time data, or jump to Pro ($45) if you work in Excel. Most retail traders never need Enterprise.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Koyfin

1. Use the Screener for Thesis Validation, Not Discovery

New users often treat Koyfin's screener like a stock picker. Instead, use it to validate ideas you already have. Define your thesis first (e.g., "profitable SaaS companies with high retention," "cyclicals near trough margins"), then build a screen to find 5-10 candidates. This yields higher-conviction ideas than running a 50-filter mega screen that returns hundreds of results you won't evaluate.

2. Combine Price Charts with Fundamental Data Overlays

Most traders look at price action in isolation. In Koyfin, overlay earnings announcements, dividend dates, and insider trades directly on your price chart. You'll often notice that price breakouts follow earnings surprises or insider buying — this is a form of confirmation that costs zero extra.

3. Create Separate Watchlists by Investment Thesis

Instead of one "watchlist" with 100 tickers, create 3-5 focused lists: "Deep Dives" (high-conviction ideas you're researching), "Monitoring" (former positions you'd buy again), "Comps" (peer companies for benchmarking). This forces discipline and makes your news feed less noisy since you're only tracking relevant tickers.

4. Set Alert Rules Around Earnings and Analyst Changes

Alerts based on price movements fire constantly and waste your attention. Instead, set alerts for events that actually matter: earnings misses, analyst downgrades, or insider transactions. These are rarer but higher-signal events that warrant immediate review.

5. Benchmark Your Holdings Weekly

Use Koyfin's fundamental dashboard to compare your holdings against peers. If your semiconductor position is growing revenue at 2% while peers grow at 8%, that's a red flag. Monthly peer comparisons catch deteriorating competitive position before the market punishes it.

6. Export Screener Results to a Master Spreadsheet

Run your main investment screens monthly, export results, and paste into a master spreadsheet with formulas that rank companies by your custom scoring system. Over time, this becomes your deal pipeline — you're not searching for ideas, you're executing on a systematic process.

7. Use the News Feed Strategically, Not Obsessively

Koyfin's news aggregation is excellent but can be addictive. Set specific times to review news (9:30am, 4pm, end of week) rather than checking all day. Filter by source credibility to avoid noise. Most news is already priced in; focus on proprietary insights, insider trades, and regulatory filings instead.

Common Koyfin Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Real-time Data Lag or Frozen Prices

If prices aren't updating, check your subscription tier. Free and Plus tiers may show 15-20 minute delays during market hours. Switch to a paid tier for true real-time data, or refresh your browser (Ctrl+Shift+R) if you're on a paid plan and still seeing stale data. Koyfin's data feeds occasionally sync issues — if a major ticker is frozen, try closing the app and logging back in.

Issue 2: Screener Returns 0 Results or Too Many

If your screen returns nothing, you're filtering too strictly. Remove one constraint at a time and rerun. If it returns 500+ results, add filters (e.g., market cap over $1B, revenue growth over 5%) to narrow it. Save working screens so you don't rebuild them daily — click "Save Screen" after you get useful results.

Issue 3: Excel Integration Not Updating

Live Excel links only work on Pro tier and above. If your spreadsheet isn't updating, check that you're pasting data as "Koyfin Link" (not as values). Verify your internet connection and that Koyfin is logged in. Excel links update once per minute during market hours; they won't refresh instantly like terminal data.

Issue 4: Overwhelmed by Too Much Data

Koyfin's depth can paralyze new users. Start with a single watchlist of 5 tickers. Build one custom screen. Set 3 alerts. Expand from there over weeks. Don't try to master all features immediately — focus on screener, watchlists, and news feed first.

Is Koyfin Worth It? Our Verdict

Koyfin delivers Bloomberg-grade data analysis for less than $50/month, making it exceptional value for serious retail investors and portfolio managers. The screener, charting, and fundamental dashboards are genuinely professional-quality. Real drawbacks are the lack of trade execution (research-only), no mobile app, and heavy focus on stocks/ETFs rather than crypto or forex. If you're a fundamental analyst, position trader, or swing trader who spends 2+ hours per week researching companies, Koyfin at $27.50-$45/month pays for itself in efficiency gains. If you trade intraday, prefer simple setups, or want an all-in-one broker, look at thinkorswim or TradingView. For deep-dive fundamental research, Koyfin is hard to beat without a Bloomberg terminal. See our full Koyfin review for detailed feature breakdowns or compare it with Koyfin vs TradingView and Koyfin vs FinViz.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
